title: In-Context Learning vs. Fine-Tuning
concept: Fine-tuning updates weights; in-context learning is purely activation-based, happening during the forward pass on a frozen model.

exercises.prompt: What physical parts of the AI network are permanently updated during fine-tuning?
exercises.explain: Fine-tuning updates weights; in-context learning is activation-based and happens during a forward pass on a frozen model.

cards.title: Does AI Ever Forget?
cards.body: Have you ever wondered if an AI is actually 'learning' when you chat with it? There are two main ways an AI learns: by changing its brain permanently, or by temporarily adapting to the clues you write in your prompt!

cards.title: Updating Weights vs. Reading Prompts
cards.body:
**Fine-Tuning** updates the actual weights (the brain connections) of the AI model. It permanently learns new patterns by training on data.

**In-Context Learning** happens when you show the AI examples directly in the prompt. The model is completely frozen; it simply reads the context and adapts its behavior on the fly during a single forward pass.

cards.title: The Chef's Kitchen
cards.body:
Imagine your AI is a master chef. 

**Fine-Tuning** is like sending the chef to a culinary school to learn a brand-new cooking style. The chef actually changes their techniques and muscle memory permanently.

**In-Context Learning** is like giving the chef a specific recipe card right before they start cooking. They don't go back to school; they just look at the card, make the meal, and immediately forget it once the dish is served!

cards.title: French Class vs. Cheat Sheet
cards.body:
Let's see both in action for translation:

• **In-Context Learning (Prompt):**
"Translate these:
Apple -> Pomme
Dog -> Chien
Cat -> [AI outputs 'Chat' here]"
*(The AI figured it out from the prompt, but its brain didn't change!)*

• **Fine-Tuning:**
You feed 10,000 French translation pairs into the training algorithm. The model's weights update, making it permanently better at French.

cards.title: The Myth of Permanent Chatting
cards.body:
**Myth:** When you give an AI five examples in a prompt, it permanently remembers them and gets smarter for the next user.

**Reality:** In-context learning is temporary! The moment your chat session ends, the AI goes back to its original state. The model is frozen, and no weights were changed.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Let's compare how the AI processes information in these two different modes.

left.text: Model is frozen. It reads examples in the prompt and uses temporary activations to answer. No brain surgery needed!

right.text: Model weights are updated. It physically changes its parameters using training algorithms to learn permanently.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is true about In-Context Learning?

options.text: It is activation-based and runs on a frozen model.
It permanently rewires the network connections.
It requires hours of retraining on supercomputers.
It deletes the old parameters to make room for new ones.

exercises.explain: In-context learning happens purely during the forward pass of a frozen model without modifying any stored weights.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Fine-tuning physically modifies the weights of the neural network.
exercises.explain: Fine-tuning changes the network's weights, which is a permanent update. In-context learning is temporary and lasts only for the duration of the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe the state of the model during in-context learning.
exercises.sentence: During in-context learning, the AI model's weights are kept completely [blank] and do not change.
exercises.explain: In-context learning does not alter the underlying model parameters; the model remains frozen and only processes the temporary clues provided in the prompt.

exercises.prompt: Identify the scenario that uses In-Context Learning.

options.text: An AI trained on 50,000 medical records to become a medical assistant.
An AI update downloaded overnight to improve its voice tone.
An AI given two examples of writing code, then asked to write a third program.

exercises.explain: The model in Option C is using in-context learning because it is given a couple of examples directly in the input text to help it complete the next task, without updating its weights.

exercises.prompt: You need to choose the best strategy for a customer service chatbot. If you choose to fine-tune the model instead of using in-context learning (prompting), what is physically happening to the AI model?

options.text: Fine-tuning is temporary, while in-context learning permanently updates the model weights.
Fine-tuning permanently changes the model's weights, while in-context learning uses a frozen model.
Both methods permanently change the underlying model weights during the process.
Neither method changes the weights, but fine-tuning requires more memory.

exercises.explain: In-Context Learning (prompting) is purely activation-based during a forward pass on a frozen model. Fine-tuning actually updates the physical weight parameters of the neural network permanently.
